# Research Agent Context Pack — `CCDash`

> **Companion artifacts:** project-purpose-and-feature-catalog (feature inventory) · project-fundamentals-and-design-context (design DNA) · project-opportunity-map (forward-looking ideas)

> **Audience.** Downstream AI agents performing research, ideation, or strategy work on this project. Optimize for density and scannability. Cross-reference the catalog and fundamentals artifacts; do not duplicate them.

## Executive Context for Researchers

CCDash is a local-first, single-tenant-by-default dashboard for orchestrating, monitoring, and forensically analyzing AI coding-agent sessions (Claude Code and Codex) against a developer's own repos, documents, and Git history. Its central architectural commitment is filesystem-as-source-of-truth: a continuously-running sync engine parses session JSONL logs, markdown (plan documents, features, progress files), and test results from disk and writes normalized rows into a SQLite cache (Postgres is an opt-in enterprise profile). Every domain entity — session, document, feature, task, test run, execution record — has a parser, a repository, and an edge in a universal link graph (`backend/db/repositories/entity_graph.py`), so cross-cutting queries like "which session drove this feature to Done?" are mechanical joins rather than bespoke pipelines.

The backend is a FastAPI app organized as **Router → Service → Repository** (strictly enforced per `CLAUDE.md`) and runs under four distinct profiles — `local`, `api`, `worker`, `test` — each with its own capability set (`watch`, `sync`, `jobs`, `auth`, `integrations`) declared in `backend/runtime/profiles.py`. The frontend is a React 19 SPA on Vite with `HashRouter`, served from repo root, state managed through a split shell of Contexts (`AppSessionContext`, `AppEntityDataContext`, `AppRuntimeContext`) plus a `DataContext` compatibility facade.

Current maturity: production-grade for the core forensic and analytics surfaces (Dashboard, Session Inspector, Project Board, Execution Workbench, Workflow Registry, Ops Panel) with recent shipped work in session-intelligence canonical storage, dependency-aware execution, telemetry exporter hardening, and SkillMeat memory-draft review. Active work on the `feat/cli-mcp-enablement-codex` branch is building a Typer-based CLI, a dual-transport MCP server, and a transport-neutral agent-query layer (Phase 1 foundation complete).

Constraints a researcher must respect: (1) local-first is a product value, not a temporary limitation — any proposal that makes CCDash require remote infrastructure is a fight; (2) the filesystem is the truth, so bypassing the sync engine desynchronizes the UI; (3) the layering convention is load-bearing; (4) feature flags default off for experimental surfaces and default on after rollout, and both UI and backend check them; (5) external services (Gemini, SAM telemetry, SkillMeat, pricing APIs) must degrade cleanly when absent; (6) upstream publishing (SkillMeat memory drafts, telemetry) requires human approval gates; (7) the team tracks incomplete work in plan/progress files under `docs/project_plans/` and `.claude/progress/` rather than inline TODOs — scanning for TODO debt will underestimate scope. Multi-tenant auth and remote execution connectors exist as PRDs only.

## Domain Glossary

| Term | Definition | Where it appears |
|---|---|---|
| Session | A single AI coding agent run, captured as a JSONL log plus derived facts | `backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py`, `backend/db/repositories/sessions.py` |
| Canonical transcript | Normalized `session_messages` rows derived from raw logs, used for semantic search and intelligence queries | `backend/services/session_transcript_contract.py`, `backend/services/session_transcript_projection.py` |
| Feature | A user-facing unit of work backed by a markdown spec, progress, and linked sessions | `backend/parsers/features.py`, `backend/db/repositories/features.py`, `components/ProjectBoard` |
| Plan document | A PRD, implementation plan, spike, or progress file parsed from markdown frontmatter | `backend/parsers/documents.py`, `docs/project_plans/` |
| Runtime profile | One of `local` / `api` / `worker` / `test`; controls which subsystems bootstrap | `backend/runtime/profiles.py` |
| Storage profile | `local` (SQLite) or `enterprise` (Postgres); bound to a capability matrix | `backend/runtime/profiles.py`, `backend/runtime/storage_contract.py` |
| Entity link | A typed edge in the universal cross-entity graph (`source_type`/`id` → `target_type`/`id`) | `backend/db/repositories/entity_graph.py`, `backend/document_linking.py` |
| Sync engine | Filesystem → cache incremental sync engine with hash tracking | `backend/db/sync_engine.py` |
| Session intelligence | Derived facts per session (churn, sentiment, scope drift) | `backend/services/session_churn_facts.py`, `backend/services/session_sentiment_facts.py`, `backend/services/session_scope_drift.py` |
| Stack recommendation | "Next best action" recommendation combining tools, models, platforms, workflows | `backend/services/stack_recommendations.py` |
| Workflow effectiveness | Success / efficiency / quality / risk ranking of workflows | `backend/services/workflow_effectiveness.py` |
| Execution gate | Policy verdict that must pass before the execution runtime launches a subprocess | `backend/services/execution_policy.py` |
| Memory draft | A SkillMeat session-memory artifact pending operator review before publish | `backend/services/integrations/skillmeat_memory_drafts.py` |
| Append-first delta | Safe transcript update format for active sessions | `backend/routers/live.py` |
| Family view | Feature lineage / sequence visualization with blocked-by chips | `components/ProjectBoard`, `components/FeatureExecutionWorkbench` |
| Agent query layer | Planned transport-neutral abstraction letting CLI/MCP/extension share one query surface | `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-003-transport-neutral-agent-query-layer.md` |
| SAM telemetry | Upstream opt-in observability export target | `backend/services/integrations/sam_telemetry_client.py`, `backend/services/integrations/telemetry_exporter.py` |

## Project Intent and Non-Goals

**Intent**:
- Provide a forensic, cost-aware dashboard for a developer's own AI coding-agent sessions `[evidence H]` (`README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`).
- Join Kanban-style project management with session forensics and document graphs in one place `[evidence H]` (`backend/document_linking.py`, `components/ProjectBoard`, `components/SessionInspector`).
- Run locally against a developer's filesystem by default, with an opt-in enterprise posture `[evidence H]` (`backend/runtime/profiles.py`, `backend/config.py`).
- Recommend next-best actions (tools, models, platforms, workflows) from observed session data `[evidence H]` (`backend/services/stack_recommendations.py`).
- Track workflow effectiveness, failure patterns, cost attribution, and scope drift as first-class intelligence `[evidence H]` (`backend/services/workflow_effectiveness.py`, `backend/services/session_scope_drift.py`).

**Explicit non-goals** (things the project deliberately does NOT do):
- **Not** a remote SaaS. Local-first is a default commitment, not a limitation `[inference H]` (`backend/runtime/profiles.py`, `backend/config.py`).
- **Not** a generic observability platform. OTEL + Prometheus + SAM export exist to serve CCDash's forensic story, not to replace Grafana/Datadog `[inference H]` (`backend/observability/otel.py`).
- **Not** multi-tenant today. Shared auth / RBAC / SSO is PRD-only; `backend/db/repositories/identity_access.py` is a stub `[evidence H]` (`docs/project_plans/PRDs/enhancements/shared-auth-rbac-sso-v1.md`).
- **Not** autonomously publishing anything upstream. SkillMeat memory drafts and telemetry export are approval-gated or opt-in `[evidence H]` (`CHANGELOG.md`, `backend/services/integrations/skillmeat_memory_drafts.py`).
- **Not** a remote execution platform. Platform connectors and SDK orchestration are planned (Execution Workbench phases 3–4) but unimplemented `[evidence H]` (`docs/project_plans/PRDs/enhancements/feature-execution-workbench-phase-3-platform-connectors-v1.md`).

## Research Guardrails

- **Architectural invariants**: Router → Service → Repository layering; filesystem-as-source-of-truth; runtime-profile capability matrix; universal `entity_links` graph; canonical `session_messages` transcript storage. Breaking any of these requires explicit discussion in a plan or ADR.
- **Product principles**: Local-first by default, enterprise opt-in; human approval gates on outbound publishing; no autonomous feature enablement; documentation co-shipped with code (`CHANGELOG.md`).
- **Operational constraints**: Single-user assumption; trust-the-project-root threat model; external services must degrade cleanly when absent; three experimental flags (`CCDASH_TEST_VISUALIZER_ENABLED`, `CCDASH_INTEGRITY_SIGNALS_ENABLED`, `CCDASH_LIVE_TEST_UPDATES_ENABLED`) default off.
- **Compatibility surfaces**: The session-log parser (`backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py`) tracks external Claude Code log format; breaking changes here cascade to intelligence, analytics, and UI. The `entity_links` graph schema is consumed by every domain.

## Priority Opportunity Areas

1. **Ship the CLI/MCP/agent-query layer end-to-end** (phase 1 foundation complete; blocks every headless or IDE surface) — supporting evidence: `docs/project_plans/adrs/adr-003-transport-neutral-agent-query-layer.md`, recent commits on `feat/cli-mcp-enablement-codex`.
2. **Refactor the load-bearing mega-files** (`backend/db/sync_engine.py`, `backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py`, `backend/routers/analytics.py`) into domain-partitioned modules — supporting evidence: all three are multi-thousand-LOC, all are on every hot path.
3. **Productize session intelligence for agent consumption** via MCP resources: expose semantic search, churn/sentiment/scope-drift facts, and stack recommendations so a coding agent can self-assess its own prior sessions — supporting evidence: `backend/services/session_intelligence_queries.py`, `backend/services/stack_recommendations.py` are already built for the UI.

## High-Risk Areas / Sensitive Assumptions

| Area | Why risky | What would need re-validation |
|---|---|---|
| `backend/db/sync_engine.py` | Single biggest file; global blast radius; incremental hash tracking is subtle | Full sync test suite, migration/drift tests, performance baseline |
| `backend/parsers/platforms/claude_code/parser.py` | Tracks an external format that evolves; breakage silently degrades intelligence | Parser fixtures for every observed log variant |
| `backend/runtime/profiles.py` capability matrix | Every feature branches on this; adding a capability touches all four profiles | Profile-by-profile integration tests |
| `entity_links` schema / `backend/document_linking.py` | Cross-feature graph; changes cascade | Link-round-trip tests per entity type |
| Feature flag behavior (`CCDASH_*`) | Flags gate both UI and backend independently | Default-on-vs-default-off matrix tests |
| SkillMeat publish flow | Approval gate is the safety net; any bypass is a product-value violation | Approval-path integration test, audit log |
| Telemetry exporter queue | Queue cap + retention protects CPU budget (<2%) | Load test per `CHANGELOG.md` 2026-03-27 |

## Suggested Research Questions

1. **What is the full data lineage from a JSONL session log to a Stack Recommendation?** — unlocks: confident MCP surface design, refactor planning.
2. **Which features actually exercise `CCDASH_STORAGE_SHARED_POSTGRES` in tests?** — unlocks: safe enterprise-profile expansion.
3. **Is `backend/db/sync_engine.py` factorable into independent per-entity sync modules?** — unlocks: biggest tech-debt win.
4. **How much of `components/ProjectBoard` (206KB) is genuinely Kanban logic vs. drill-down modal plumbing?** — unlocks: frontend refactor target.
5. **Can session intelligence queries be served directly over the agent-query layer without going through `backend/routers/analytics.py`?** — unlocks: MCP productization.
6. **Which session-intelligence facts are cheap to derive incrementally vs. require full-session reprocessing?** — unlocks: live-update scope.
7. **Is there observable drift between default-on and default-off feature flags and the shipped UI?** — unlocks: confidence in experimental surfaces.

## Proof-of-Concept Snippets

### Snippet 1: Runtime profiles as capability matrix

**File**: `backend/runtime/profiles.py:1-40` `[evidence H]`

```python
"""Runtime profile definitions for CCDash process modes."""
from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Literal

RuntimeProfileName = Literal["local", "api", "worker", "test"]
StorageProfileName = Literal["local", "enterprise"]


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class RuntimeCapabilities:
    watch: bool
    sync: bool
    jobs: bool
    auth: bool
    integrations: bool


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class RuntimeProfile:
    name: RuntimeProfileName
    capabilities: RuntimeCapabilities
    recommended_storage_profile: StorageProfileName
    description: str
```

**What it shows**: The profile system is a tiny, frozen, typed capability matrix — every feature must answer "does this profile have `watch`/`sync`/`jobs`/`auth`/`integrations`?" before it decides whether to bootstrap. This is the single most load-bearing abstraction in the backend and it fits in ~40 lines.

### Snippet 2: Local entrypoint is a 13-line delegation

**File**: `backend/runtime/bootstrap_local.py:1-13` `[evidence H]`

```python
"""Local convenience bootstrap."""
from __future__ import annotations

from fastapi import FastAPI

from backend.runtime.bootstrap import build_runtime_app


def build_local_app() -> FastAPI:
    return build_runtime_app("local")


app = build_local_app()
```

**What it shows**: Each runtime profile's bootstrap is a thin shim over a single shared `build_runtime_app(profile_name)` factory. The heavy lifting (DI, routing, startup tasks) is centralized, which is why adding a profile is cheap but *changing* what a profile does is expensive.

### Snippet 3: Runtime capability matrix dictates profile behavior

**File**: `backend/runtime/profiles.py:28-77` `[evidence H]`

```python
_RUNTIME_PROFILES: dict[RuntimeProfileName, RuntimeProfile] = {
    "local": RuntimeProfile(
        name="local",
        capabilities=RuntimeCapabilities(
            watch=True, sync=True, jobs=True, auth=False, integrations=True,
        ),
        recommended_storage_profile="local",
        description="Desktop-style local profile with automatic sync, watcher, and in-process jobs.",
    ),
    "api": RuntimeProfile(
        name="api",
        capabilities=RuntimeCapabilities(
            watch=False, sync=False, jobs=False, auth=True, integrations=True,
        ),
        recommended_storage_profile="enterprise",
        description="HTTP-serving profile without incidental watcher or startup sync work.",
    ),
}
```

**What it shows**: The four profiles carry different capability flags in a frozen dataclass. Every feature must check its profile's bits; this is why `local` can do everything solo while `api` delegates sync/jobs to `worker`. The profile matrix is the backbone of multi-runtime support.

## Unknowns Requiring Human Validation

1. Where CI runs and what gates PRs (no `.github/workflows/` surfaced in this pass).
2. Whether a VSCode extension exists outside this repo per ADR-001.
3. Which embedding provider backs `backend/db/repositories/session_embeddings.py` and under what license.
4. Whether the enterprise filesystem ingestion flag is fully wired or a staged rollout flag.
5. Operator expectations for SkillMeat review cadence (minutes, hours, days?).
6. Whether CCDash is ever bundled as a desktop app (Electron/Tauri) or strictly localhost web.
